title: maquira guianensis subsp. costaricana
intro: Maquira guianensis subsp. costaricana, also known as Guayacan, is a tropical tree native to Central America and the Caribbean, belonging to the family Moraceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Maquira guianensis subsp. costaricana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the tree's bark and roots are used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
